questions.question: Consider a game where two players, A and B, decide whether to invest in a project. Player A has a 60% chance of success and player B has a 40% chance of success. A player will gain $100 if the project succeeds and lose $100 if it fails. Assuming both players can randomize their investments, find a mixed strategy equilibrium for this game. (3 marks)

Suppose two players, X and Y, are playing a game where they decide whether to send a message (M) or not send a message (N). Player X receives $10 if Y sends a message and $0 otherwise. Player Y receives $20 if X sends a message and $0 otherwise. Assuming both players can randomize their messages, find a mixed strategy equilibrium for this game. (5 marks)

A sender and a receiver are communicating through a noisy channel, where the probability of a message being sent correctly is 0.8. The sender has a 0.6 probability of sending a 'yes' or 'no' message. Assuming both the sender and receiver can randomize their responses, find a mixed strategy equilibrium for this game. (5 marks)
